anti_claims: This organ JSON seed does not flip source authority away from core/organ_atlas.json and core/organ_registry.json.
Required paper/mechanism/code links prove declared structural references only, not runtime correctness or release readiness.
Selective concept, principle, axiom, and sibling-organ edges are not inferred from prose, specialty, family, or evidence class.
Absent selective organ edges are residual pressure, not evidence that no neighbours exist.
Generated markdown, graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

code_loci.role: Runtime and receipt-writing locus for durable work-landing replay validation, exported bundle source-module digest checks, negative-case enforcement, authority ceilings, and body-free public receipts.

mechanism_refs.edge_justification: The organ's runtime, first-wave fixture, exported work-landing bundle, copied macro source-body manifest, acceptance receipt, runtime-shell bundle validator, and focused tests validate public work-landing replay rows, validation-before-commit ordering, metadata-blocked closeout, HEAD-advance evidence, source-module digest checks, and body-free receipt boundaries without mutating Git or upgrading replay rows into live commit authority.

omitted: runtime outputs beyond named public receipts
selective doctrine neighbours not named by atlas source rows
omission_receipt.reason: Organ instance preserves public-safe atlas/registry fields. Required links resolve only when source ids or paths resolve; selective law/concept/principle edges remain residual unless source evidence names ids.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Populate missing paper/mechanism/code links from source evidence, then bind concept/principle/axiom/wires_to edges only when ids are named.

organ_payload.claim_ceiling: It validates only the declared public work-landing contract over recorded rows. It is evidence for fixture-local closeout mechanics, not for live Git side effects, unrelated-path staging, private body export, service operation, or distribution clearance.

source_atlas_row.agent_gloss: An agent runs this over recorded work-landing rows to confirm they satisfy the declared contract (each row cites owned claimed paths and owner-native validation refs; validation precedes the commit attempt; a "committed-landed" claim carries a declared HEAD-before != HEAD-after advance; metadata-blocked rows capture a blocker ref; Work Ledger closeout is cited) and to confirm the fixture's nine negative rows are each rejected with their expected error codes. It validates the recorded contract only; it does not execute Git or prove real-world landedness.
source_atlas_row.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public work-landing contract over recorded rows. It is evidence for fixture-local closeout mechanics, not for live Git side effects, unrelated-path staging, private body export, service operation, or distribution clearance.

code_loci.role: Runtime and receipt-writing locus for durable work-landing replay validation, exported bundle source-module digest checks, negative-case enforcement, authority ceilings, and body-free public receipts.

source_atlas_row.human_gloss: This checks recorded examples of an agent finishing a piece of work the careful way: each example must name the exact files it claims to have touched, show that validation was recorded before any commit was attempted, and only label itself "committed" if the example also records the repository's HEAD moving. It also checks that blockers and ledger closeout were captured. The check shows whether each recorded work-claim carries the required evidence and ordering, rather than being an unbacked chat boast. It judges the recorded claims against the contract; it does not run Git or prove that any commit truly landed in a real repository.

source_atlas_row.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret_exclusion_scan, receipts, schemas). Doctrine-lattice wires_to additions in this row are source-derived from core/mechanism_sources.json upstream_of rows for the organ's hosted mechanism(s); they name structural sibling navigation only, not live invocation, release authority, or proof of runtime integration.
